name: Berry Companion
shortDescription: Open Berry tasks, approvals, and page capture from a Chrome side panel.
singlePurpose: Berry Companion connects the browser to the user's configured Berry account or local Berry Desktop host so the user can attach the current page to a task and approve agent actions.
detailedDescription: Berry Companion is the Chrome side panel for Berry. It shows your Berry task list, current thread, composer-lite, pending approvals, and explicit page-capture actions.

Page content is captured only after the user clicks Selection, Full page, or the Send to Berry context-menu item. Full-page capture requests per-site permission through Chrome before reading page text.
The extension can connect to a signed-in Berry platform endpoint or to Berry Desktop through Chrome native messaging after the user enables the desktop security setting with their extension ID.
The extension does not sell data, inject ads, mine browsing history, or transmit page content anywhere except the Berry endpoint or local desktop host selected by the user.

permissionsJustification.activeTab: Read the active tab only after an explicit user action.
permissionsJustification.contextMenus: Offer the user-initiated Send to Berry command.
permissionsJustification.nativeMessaging: Connect to Berry Desktop when the user enables the desktop bridge.
permissionsJustification.notifications: Show approval and capture status notifications.
permissionsJustification.sidePanel: Render Berry in Chrome's side panel.
permissionsJustification.scripting: Capture selected or full-page text after active-tab or per-origin permission is granted.
permissionsJustification.storage: Store pending page-capture payloads in Chrome session storage.

hostPermissionsJustification: No broad host permissions are requested by default. Optional http/https origin permission is requested per site only when the user chooses full-page capture.

privacy.dataLeavesDevice: Only page context, task messages, approvals, and device/session metadata sent to the Berry endpoint or local desktop host configured by the user.
